observations.reason: Still, not all PPEs were physically counted for the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer (GSO) to facilitate the registration and transfer of the title of the land properties from the previous owners to the Municipality of Maragondon; b. Municipal Accountant and the GSO to reconcile the discrepancy of P39,664,730.00 between their records to come up with the correct value of land; c. BAC and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the earlier enumerated supporting documents on the land purchased in CY 2020, otherwise the subject transaction will be suspended in audit; and d. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain complete Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC) for land account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor commented that one of their problems on the titling of these lots was the funding source, nonetheless, together with the Municipal Accountant, they will coordinate to find a suitable solution to these problems to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: Titles were still not registered and transferred in the name of the Municipality. Documents were still not submitted to the Audit Team. No RPLCs were prepared by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials as
inventory items only those items actually delivered and received and record as
expenses only those items actually expended/consumed during the accounting
period using the perpetual inventory method;
b. Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Custodian to maintain supplies
ledger cards and stock cards for each commodity/stock;
c. GSO and all other property custodian to consolidate the RIS on a weekly basis
using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and furnish the
Municipal Accountant with copies thereof for recording in the books of cost of
issued items; and
d. Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories semi-annually,
and to prepare and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories
(RPCI) to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer (GSO) to
facilitate the registration and transfer of the title of the land properties from the
previous owners to the Municipality of Maragondon; and
b. direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO, with the help of the Municipal
Assessor’s record of land, to identify the lots that caused the discrepancy of
P37,579,730.00 between their records to come up with the correct and reliable
value of land in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete annual physical count of all
properties of the Municipality, which should include prior years’ acquisition and
not only those acquired in the current year and thereafter reconcile the Report
of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the
accounting records; Should reconciliation of the RPCPPE and accounting
records appeared to still be highly difficult or improbable, we recommend that
the Municipality avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE afforded under COA
Circular No. 2020-006;
a. the Municipal Accounting Office and General Services Office to maintain
complete PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for each category of PPE, including
therein the dates of acquisition, description, reference, accumulated
depreciation and other necessary information on the property;
b. the Municipal Accountant to affect the correcting entries for the donated
property to the barangay; and
c. the General Services Office to renew and update the ARE, property tags and
property numbers of all PPEs, consistent with the provisions of MNGAS for
LGUs.

a. to revert the dormant payables amounting to P2,123,933.49 to the
unappropriated surplus;
b. in coordination with the GSO, to submit to the Audit Team duly received
purchase orders and/or perfected contracts, Delivery Receipts,
Acknowledgement Receipts, Contractor’s Statement of Worked Accomplished,
Inspection and Acceptance Report (AIR) and/or other equivalent documents to
support the recording of the remaining unclaimed/unpaid Accounts Payable in
CYs 2020 and 2021. Otherwise, revert all unsupported Accounts Payable to the
General or Special Education Funds of the Municipality;
c. to refrain from recording Accounts Payable merely on the basis of Obligation
Requests or CAFOA and ensure instead that all supporting documents that will
prove that there are actual deliveries of goods or services or work
accomplishments are attached to the claims; and
d. prepare and draw the necessary adjusting entries to correctly classify the
terminal leave benefits and financial assistance amounting to P729,953.47 and
P140,000.00 to their respective accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. conduct a review, analysis and reconciliation of all dormant accounts by
identifying the nature, source and purpose of the funds received and/or
collected by the Municipality; and coordinate with the source agencies (SA) if
these amounts will be returned to the SA or be reverted back to the
unappropriated surplus of the General Fund; and

e. prepare and draw the necessary adjusting entries to correctly classify the total
amount of P6,871,768.59 to their respective accounts; and thereafter, properly
classify every transactions in accordance with the RCALGU for proper
presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the complete
financial statements, copies of general and subsidiary ledgers and other
necessary accounting records and reports on time to facilitate the audit of
Municipal accounts; and
b. General Services Officer (GSO) to submit the reports on physical count of
inventories not later than January 31, and of property, plant and equipment not
later than July 31 and January 31 of each year as prescribed by the NGAS
Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to facilitate the liquidation of all outstanding cash
advances at year-end and henceforth, adopt appropriate control measures to
prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advances in the succeeding years;
b. Municipal Treasurer to increase the amount of bond of accountable officers who
were granted large amount of cash advances pursuant to Treasury Circular
No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009 to protect the interest of the Municipal
Government, otherwise, limit the amount of their cash advances up to the
maximum cash accountability of their fidelity bond. Likewise, ensure that only
bonded and authorized official/employees are granted cash advances;
c. Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of granting cash advance to an
elected official except in connection with official travel;
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each
accountable officer granted with cash advances; and
d. Comply with RCALGU in recording and classifying the advances granted by the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the
MSWDO to review the SEA-K accounts to date and determine the probability of their
collection, taking into consideration the age of the loans and the whereabouts of the
beneficiaries and to recommend whether there is a need to initiate a request to write-
off the receivables from the COA.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO to study
comprehensively all issuances of the DSWD on SEA-K so that the SEA-K program
may be properly implemented by the Municipality and that pertinent transactions may
be correctly documented and recorded.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO to renew the registration
with the LTO of the 11 vehicles owned by the Municipality in compliance with
Section 5 of Batas Pambansa Bilang 74, follow-up the release of the number plates of
the vehicles and prioritize the document transfer of the three vehicles to the name of
the Municipality of Maragondon.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
review the balance of the Due to NGAs account, particularly the amount of
P3,305,152.80, that had been dormant or the purpose of which had already been
achieved by the Municipality, make the necessary adjustments in the books and
immediately return the net amount to the Bureau of Treasury, except when authority to
use the fund for other purposes is secured from the source agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. BPLO to require concerned suppliers to provide written explanation, copy
furnished the COA Audit Team, why they did not fully report their gross sales for
purpose of computation of their business taxes and to institute appropriate
action against erring suppliers, if warranted;
b. BPLO to require all suppliers to submit a Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts
and Sales and/or the Certified Copy of the Income Tax Return to serve as basis
in the computation of their business taxes, in addition to the list of payments
made to them by the Municipality pertinent to their contracts with the latter and
the barangays in the preceding calendar year, if any, that may be provided by
54
the Municipal Accountant; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to enforce against concerned suppliers/contractors the
collection of underpayments of business taxes totaling P381,457.77 including
penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the
Municipal Revenue Code, otherwise Notice of Charge will be issued by the Audit
Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate Receipts from Market Operations
account in recording the income from MPM;
b. Market Supervisor to strictly enforce the provisions of Chapter V of the Revenue
Code of the Municipality of Maragondon, Cavite series of 2019, and the
“Kasunduan Para sa Pag-Upa ng Pwesto,” on the application, award, transfer
and sub-Lease of stalls, and to recommend and/or impose sanctions to violators
such as demanding that the transferred stalls be vacated by the new occupants
except when the transfer Is approved by proper authorities;
c. Market Supervisor to prepare and/or update the records of the “awardees” or
stallholders, including the preparation and execution of the Market Award
Agreement and/or the renewal of the “Kasunduan Para sa Pag-Upa ng Pwesto,”
to remind the stallholders of the terms and conditions for the award and their
responsibilities as an “awardee”.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the concerned officials and personnel to exercise prudence in the
utilization of resources intended for meals and snacks during official meetings,
programs/projects and activities and observe the limitations or prohibitions to
reception and entertainment as prescribed by Section 343 of RA 7160;
b. direct the Municipal Officials and personnel to charge the costs of meals and
snacks for entertaining visitors/guests of their corresponding offices to their
monthly representation allowances, which were granted to them for that very
purpose;
c. direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge the
amount of discretionary expenses to the appropriate Extraordinary and
60
Miscellaneous Expenses account; and
d. limit the amount spent for discretionary/extraordinary expenses to the amount
equivalent to 2 per cent of the actual receipts of Real Property Taxes of the next
preceding calendar year, to avoid suspension or possible disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat and the
General Services Officer (GSO) to consolidate the PPMPs submitted by all end-
users/departments for the year and include all requirements for common-use
supplies to the APP-CSE to be submitted to the DBM-PS and henceforth procure the
same to the DBM-PS in compliance with the mandate of Administrative Order No. 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC and all other concerned
officials responsible for the conduct of the seminar-workshop to submit written
explanation/justifications on choosing to conduct the workshop in Puerto Princesa,
Palawan which resulted to the incurrence of P1,086,000.00 for airfare and
accommodation of participants.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the BAC
responsible for the award to submit written justifications why the contract was
awarded to JVLA Easy Travel and Tours although the latter was not able to submit
complete and valid eligibility requirements and henceforth, should have been
disqualified by the BAC and why pre-bid conference was not conducted.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
submit to the Audit Team the names and office of the 28 participants, Certificates of
Appearance/Training of all the 41 participants and the approved course/seminar
agenda or program of activities.

Should the above requirements be not complied with by concerned officials or the
submitted explanation/justification and documents be not satisfactory or acceptable
to the Audit Team after evaluation, the disbursements amounting to P1,086,000.00 will
be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and other concerned officials to make sure that PPAs
charged against the 20% Development Plan are well-planned and procurement-and-
implementation-ready to meet their target dates of completion and thus benefits
therefrom may be timely enjoyed by the intended end-users.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Accountant to review the RPT and SET Receivables accounts and make sure that their balances are always equal at the end of the year; b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that set-up of collections from RPT/SET were properly recorded in the books in compliance with Sections 20, Chapter 3, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), as amended by the Government Auditing Manual for LGUs c. Municipal Accountant to rectify correct the entries made in CY 2020 and in previous years, if applicable, on transactions involving collections of Real Property Taxes (RPT) and Special Education Taxes (SET) applying the recommended correcting entries provided in Annex B; and d. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and investigate other causes of the net deficiencies of P24,788,221.55 between their records and make the necessary adjustments to establish the correct balances of the subject receivables.
observations.managementAction: RPT and SET Receivables were reviewed and checked. Correcting entries were made in CY 2020 and also made during the year.
observations.reason: The difference between the RPT and SET was due to the reflected amounts per reconciliation with the Province.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials as inventory items and record as expenses only those items actually expended/consumed during the accounting period using the perpetual inventory method; b. Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain supplies ledger cards and stock cards for each commodity/stock; c. GSO to prepare RIS Requisition Issue Slip issued upon release of inventories to end-users and to consolidate the issuances on a weekly basis using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and furnish the Municipal Accountant with copies thereof for recording in the books of cost of issued items; d. GSO to conduct physical count of inventories semi-annually, and to prepare and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year; and e. Municipal Accountant to take up in the books the actual count of inventories on hand as of December 31, 2020 as follows: Debit - Office Supplies Inventory for P398,597.78, Other Supplies and Materials Inventory for P170,666.00 and credit Prior Period Adjustment for P569,263.78
observations.managementAction: Purchases were recorded as Inventories. The GSO submitted inventory reports at year-end. Journal entry for the year was prepared for the adjustment.
observations.reason: Perpetual inventory method was still not implemented. There were not supplies ledger cards and stock cards being maintained; SSMIs were not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant: a. in coordination with the GSO, to submit to the Audit Team Delivery Receipts, Acknowledgement Receipts, Contractor’s Statement of Worked Accomplished, Inspection and Acceptance Report (AIR) and/or other equivalent documents to support the recording of the remaining unclaimed/unpaid Accounts Payable in CYs 2020 and 2021. Otherwise, revert all unsupported Accounts Payable to the General or Special Education Funds of the Municipality; b. to refrain from recording Accounts Payable merely on the basis of Obligation Requests or CAFOA and ensure instead that all supporting documents that will prove that there are actual deliveries of goods or services or work accomplishments are attached to the claims; c. GSO to make sure that Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) are properly accomplished, including the dates the goods were delivered and inspected; and d. Municipal Accountant to record the unclaimed terminal leave credits, financial and educational assistance to their proper accounts to avoid overstating the Accounts Payable and understating the Leave Benefits Payable and Other Payables, using the following adjusting entries: Debit - Accounts Payable by P2,951,032.85, Credit - Leave Benefits Payable by P2,101,032.85 and Other Payables by P850,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that they will comply with the auditor’s recommendations. Also, she already submitted to the audit team the journal entries to revert and adjust the Accounts Payable accounts as compliance with the recommendations.
observations.reason: Accounts Payables were still being recorded despite absence of proof of deliveries. Not all Accounts Payable were adjusted for the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Assistant Municipal Accountant to: a. stop recording cash advances directly to the expense account but instead record the grant to the appropriate advances accounts; b. make sure that all liquidation reports are submitted and properly prepared; c. maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each accountable officer granted with cash advances; and d. submit to the Office of the Auditor the voucher and liquidation report of cash advance for the payment of financial assistance to DepEd personnel in December 2020, under check number 896376 amounting to P670,500.00, otherwise, this will be suspended in audit. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Assistant Municipal Treasurer to avoid using cash advances for purposes other than for what they were intended.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that they already stopped granting advances to Mr. Marcos B. Andalajao and will comply with the auditor’s other recommendations on cash advances. She also submitted the voucher and liquidation report for the cash advance in question.
observations.reason: Cash advances for electricity expenses were still used for financial assistance. Subsidiary ledgers per accountable officer were still not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) MSWDO to submit Justifications to the COA Audit Team on why ten Municipal employees or job order personnel were included as ESP-SAP beneficiaries when they were expressly excluded under Item VI-B of DSWD MC No.09, series of 2020; and b) Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MSWDO to submit to the COA the relevant certifications, the properly identified or labeled photos of the beneficiaries and photocopies of their identification cards with specimen signatures required under Items VII(A.1) and VIII(B.4) of DSWD MC No. 09, series of 2020 or valid justifications, for evaluation of the Audit Team, on why the documents could not be produced, otherwise the transactions will be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted the required justifications and documents as requested.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to request Wabalak Live and Dressed Chicken and Jidan Agrich Harvest Corporation to submit their Mayor’s or Business Permits and Income or Business Tax Returns and issue Official Receipts for the amounts received from the Municipality of Maragondon, and pay the withholding taxes amounting to P79,410.60 and P47,872.00, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant replied that the amount of P79,410.60 was already paid and P47,872.00 was paid in CY 2022.
observations.reason: The amount of P79,410.60 was already paid. The amount of P47,872.00 was paid in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the Office/Travel Orders or any equivalent document from the Municipal Mayor showing the authority of the personnel/workers who claimed COVID-19 hazard pay to report for work during the ECQ and Modified ECQ; and b) HRMO to require the concerned personnel who received COVID-19 hazard pay to prepare and submit the Daily Time Records (DTRs) duly reviewed and approved by their immediate supervisors.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents were prepared and submitted. HRMO required the concerned personnel to submit DTRs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the LDRRMO to refrain from charging against the LDRRMF programs, projects and activities that are not related to the DRRM activities as provided under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1; and b. cause the transfer from the General Fund to the Trust Fund – Trust Liability - DRRM the partial amount transferred representing improperly charged expenditures to the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality transferred the partial amount to the Trust Fund-DRRM account. The LDRRMO was instructed to avoid charging unrelated items to the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: There were still balances that need to be transferred to the Trust Fund-DRRM account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor limit the utilization of the SEF budget only to programs, projects and activities defined under Section 4 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2017 and cause the restoration of the amount charged against the General Fund amounting to P1,613,197.56 to the SEF.
observations.managementAction: The SEF was utilized for its intended purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO), BAC Chairperson and the Municipal Accountant submit the written justification to the COA Audit Team, for further evaluation, on why the Municipality did not comply with the conditions and requirements of GPPB Resolution 023-2007, otherwise the amount paid under the extended contract amounting to P3,456,000.00 will be suspended or disallowed in audit. Henceforth, ensure that the Municipality strictly comply with the guidelines prescribed by the GPPB on the extension of all contracts for general support services, especially for garbage services.
observations.managementAction: For the year, the Municipality complied and bid the procurement of general support services. The Municipality will ensure compliance with GPPB guidelines in future contract extensions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the PPMPs submitted by all end-users/departments and prepare a comprehensive, workable and realistic APP using the GPPB prescribed format for his approval and ensure that all procurements of goods and services are based on this APP; and to stop the practice of purchasing through reimbursements and strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 by ensuring that all payments to suppliers, contractors and other payees are made in checks, except when it can be clearly shown that it is impossible, impractical or difficult to pay the suppliers by checks, to ensure compliance with BIR Revenue Memorandum No. 23-2014 and RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Some offices still used reimbursements to procure supplies and PPE. The Municipality will require compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and prepare consolidated PPMPs.
observations.reason: PPMPs were still not consolidated for the year. Some offices still used reimbursements for procurements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to immediately: a. submit to the Audit Team all contracts and supporting documents relative to the 24 infrastructure projects and furnish copies of all the contracts entered into by the Municipality, together with supporting documents, within five working days from execution to facilitate timely and relevant audit; and b. make sure that all Notices to Proceed, contracts and all other attachments bear dates of approval or receipt for proper monitoring of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The Notices to Proceed were dated for the year. The Municipality will submit copies of contracts with complete supporting documents.
observations.reason: Copies of contracts with complete supporting documents were still not submitted to the Audit Team at the time of reporting; Notices to Proceed were dated for the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account in the General Fund for Kawayang Maragondon Economic Enterprise and to prepare a complete set of subsidiary ledger in order to be able to generate financial statements that would show whether the economic enterprise is viable.
observations.managementAction: The management has yet to fully established records and books to closely monitor the financial position and performance of KMEE.
observations.reason: The management has yet to fully established records and books to closely monitor the financial position and performance of KMEE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all properties of
the Municipality as of date and not only those acquired in the current year and
thereafter reconcile the reports with the accounting records;
b. Municipal Accountant to make sure that the balances of the PPE in the
Statement of Financial Position are equal or reconciled with the General
Ledger and PPELC balances;
c. Municipal Accounting Office and General Services Office to maintain complete
PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for each category of PPE, including therein the
dates of acquisition, description, reference, accumulated depreciation and
other necessary information on the property.

Management’s Comment:
The Management commented that they will comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to review the RPT and SET Receivables accounts and
make sure that their balances are always equal at the end of the year;
b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that set-up of and collections from RPT/SET
Receivables were properly recorded in the books in compliance with Sections
20, Chapter 3, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), as amended by the
Government Auditing Manual for LGUs
c. Municipal Accountant to rectify correct the entries made in CY 2020 and also in
previous years, if applicable, on transactions involving collections of Real
Property Taxes (RPT) and Special Education Taxes (SET) applying the
recommended correcting entries provided in Annex B; and
d. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and investigate
other causes of the net deficiencies of P24,788,221.55 between their records
and make the necessary adjustments to establish the correct balances of the
subject receivables.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant had started to comply with the recommendations and journal
entries were being prepared.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials as
Inventory items and record as expenses only those items actually
expended/consumed during the accounting period using the perpetual
inventory method;
b. Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain supplies ledger cards and
stock cards for each commodity/stock;
c. GSO to prepare Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) for issuances of
supplies/materials and to consolidate the issuances on a weekly basis using
the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and furnish the
Municipal Accountant with copies thereof for recording in the books of cost of
issued items;
d. GSO to conduct physical count of inventories semi-annually, and to prepare
and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the
Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year; and
e. Municipal Accountant to take up in the books the actual count of inventories
on hand as of December 31, 2020 as follows:
Office Supplies Inventory 398,597.78
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory 170,666.00
Prior Period Adjustment 569,263.78

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant and concerned officials commented that they will comply with
the Auditor’s recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO to submit to the Audit
Team Delivery Receipts, Acknowledgement Receipts, Contractor’s Statement
of Worked Accomplished, Inspection and Acceptance Report (AIR) and/or
other equivalent documents to support the recording of Accounts Payable in
CY 2020 and prior years totaling P11,893,705.80. Otherwise, revert all
unsupported Accounts Payable to the General or Special Education Funds of
the Municipality;
b. Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording Accounts Payable merely on
the basis of Obligation Requests and ensure instead that all supporting
38
documents that will prove that there are actual deliveries of goods or
services or work accomplishments are attached to the claims;
c. GSO to make sure that Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) are
properly accomplished, including the dates the goods were delivered and
inspected;
d. Municipal Accountant to record the unclaimed terminal leave credits,
financial and educational assistance to their proper accounts to avoid
overstating the Accounts Payable and understating the Leave Benefits
Payable and Other Payables, using the following adjusting entries:
Accounts Payable P2,951,032.85
Leave Benefits Payable P2,101,032.85
Other Payables 850,000.00

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials and personnel commented that
they will comply with the recommendations and that journal entries were being prepared.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer (GSO) to facilitate the
registration and transfer of the title of the land properties from the previous
owners to the Municipality of Maragondon;
b. Municipal Accountant and the GSO to reconcile the discrepancy of
P39,664,730.00 between their records to come up with the correct value of
land;
c. BAC and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the
earlier enumerated lacking supporting documentson the land purchased in
CY 2020 , otherwise the subject transaction will be suspended in audit; and
d. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain complete Real Property
Ledger Cards (RPLC) for land account.

Management’s Comment:
Management commented that they will comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. stop recording cash advances directly to the expense account but instead
record the grant to the appropriate advances accounts;
b. make sure that all liquidation reports are dated, Subsidiary Ledgers for each
accountable officer granted with cash advances are maintained; and
c. submit to the Office of the Auditor the disbursement voucher and liquidation
report of cash advance for the payment of financial assistance to DepEd
personnel in December 2020, under check number 896376 amounting to
P670,500.00, otherwise, this will be suspended in audit.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Assistant Municipal
Treasurer to avoid using cash advances for purposes other than for what they
were intended.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant mentioned that they will comply with the Auditor’s
recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. MSWDO to submit justification to the COA Audit Team on why ten Municipal
government employees or job order personnel were included as ESP-SAP
beneficiaries when they were expressly excluded under Item VI-B of DSWD
MC No.09, series of 2020;
b. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MSWDO to submit to the COA
the relevant certifications, the properly identified or labeled photos of the
beneficiaries and photocopies of their identification cards with specimen
signatures required under Items VII(A.1) and VIII(B.4) of DSWD MC No. 09,
series of 2020 or valid justifications, for evaluation of the Audit Team, on
why the documents could not be produced, otherwise the transactions will
be suspended in audit.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant commented that they will comply with the recommendations
and will submit the required justification and lacking documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to request Wabalak Live and Dressed
Chicken and Jidan Agrich Harvest Corporation to submit their Mayor’s or
Business Permits and Income or Business Tax Returns, issue Official Receipts for
the amounts received from the Municipality of Maragondon, and pay the
withholding taxes amounting to P79,410.60 and P47,872.00, respectively.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant replied that Wabalak Live and Dressed Chicken already
returned the amount of P79,410.60 and the amount was remitted to the BIR. However,
they were still waiting for Jidan Agrich Harvest Corporation to do the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the Office/Travel Orders or
any equivalent document from the Municipal Mayor showing the authority of
the personnel/workers who claimed COVID-19 hazard pay to report for work
during the ECQ and Modified ECQ; and
b. HRMO to require the concerned personnel who received COVID-19 hazard pay
to prepare and submit the Daily Time Records (DTRs) duly reviewed and
approved by their immediate supervisors.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant commented that they will submit the required documents to
the Audit Team and will comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the LDRRMO to refrain from charging against the LDRRMF programs,
projects and activities that are not related to the DRRM activities as provided
under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated
March 25, 2013, in this case, the garbage collection and hauling services;
and
b. cause the transfer from the General Fund to the Trust Fund – Trust Liability -
DRRM the amount of P3,456,000.00 representing improperly charged
expenditures to the LDRRMF.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant mentioned that for CY 2021, the budget for waste hauling and
collection was already charged against the MENRO funds. They promised to return or
transfer the amount of P3,456,000.00 to the Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the LSB limit the utilization of the
SEF budget only to programs, projects and activities defined under Section 4 of
the DepEd-DBM-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2017 and cause the restoration of the amount
charged against the SEF from the General Fund amounting to P1,613,197.56.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant promised to comply with the Auditor’s recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Environment and National
Resources Officer (MENRO), BAC Chairperson and the Municipal Accountant
submit written justification to the COA Audit Team, for further evaluation, why
the Municipality did not comply with the conditions and requirements of GPPB
Resolution 023-2007, otherwise the amount paid under the extended contract
amounting to P3,456,000.00 will be suspended or disallowed in audit.

Henceforth, ensure that the Municipality strictly comply with the guidelines
prescribed by the GPPB on the extension of all contracts for general support
services, more especially for garbage collection and hauling services.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipality stated that they will comply with the Auditor’s recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to
consolidate the PPMPs submitted by all end-users/departments and prepare a
comprehensive, workable and realistic APP using the GPPB prescribed format for
his approval and ensure that all procurements of goods and services are based on
this APP; and

Further, we strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned
personnel to stop the practice of purchasing through reimbursements and strictly
comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 by ensuring that all payments to suppliers,
contractors and other payees are made in checks, except when it can be clearly
shown that it is impossible, impractical or difficult to pay the suppliers by checks,
to ensure compliance with BIR Revenue Memorandum No. 23-2014 and RA 9184.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipality promised to comply with the Auditor’s recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to
immediately:
a. submit to the Audit Team all contracts and supporting documents relative to
the 24 infrastructure projects and henceforth, furnish the Auditor with copies
of all contracts entered into by the Municipality, together with supporting
documents, within five working days from execution to facilitate timely and
51
relevant audit; and
b. make sure that all contracts, Notice to Proceed and all other attachments
bear dates of approval or receipt for proper monitoring of the projects.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipality stated that they will comply with the Auditor’s recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that collections from RPT/SET Receivables were properly recorded in the books in compliance with Sections 19.b and 20, Chapter 3, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs); and b. Require the Municipal Accountant to rectify and reconstruct the incorrect entries made in CY 2019 and also in previous years, if applicable, on transactions involving collections of Real Property Taxes (RPT) and Special Education Taxes (SET) applying the recommended correcting entries provided.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the adjusting entries to correct the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the Municipal Accountant to work back and recognize all Infrastructure Assets of the Municipality as well as to prepare all the necessary Adjusting Journal Entries; b. require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to derecognize replaced parts of the Infrastructure Assets and Buildings and Other Structures in accordance with PPSAS No. 17 (paragraphs 82-87); and c. require the Municipal Accountant to henceforth religiously record, evaluate and qualify items to be recognized as part of the PPE account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the adjusting entries to correct the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the Municipal Accountant to observe the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases and issuances in compliance with the provisions of Section 114 of Chapter 7 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), Volume I and Section 44 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 12; b. require the Inventory Custodians of office supplies and drugs and medicines to submit summary of supplies/drugs and medicines issued weekly and report of physical count of inventories every July 31 and January 31 as provided in Chapter 7 of the MNGAS; and c. require the Municipal Accountant to henceforth prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to record the balances of inventories on hand as of December 31, 2019.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and concerned officials and personnel commented that they will comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to facilitate the preparation of complete LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) covering current year LDRRMF and unexpended balances from the previous years; and b. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry voucher to revert back to the general fund the unutilized LDDRMF balances for Calendar Years 2013 and 2014 to be used for social services projects, programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality included the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in their budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligating and charging LDRRMF for expenditures which are not directly related to the implementation of disaster risk management activities and ensure strict compliance with the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1. It is recommended that administrative and operating expenses be charged instead against the respective General Fund appropriations of the office concerned as provided under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM- CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Not Reiterated in Implemented Observation No. 10, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Municipal Accountant to record the PPE in the books only after delivery of the items purchased; b. instruct the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to continue their efforts in reconciling the noted variances to establish the correct book balances of the PPE accounts; and c. require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the balances of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant Implemented the adjusting entries to correct the balances of PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate bonding of the Inventory Storekeeper and Property Officer, in compliance with the provisions of Section 101 of PD 1445 and Sec. 4.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The officers were properly bonded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Market Administrator/Municipal Treasurer to submit the Schedule of Tenants with delinquent accounts to the Municipal Accountant; b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to pass the necessary Journal Entry Voucher based on the submitted schedule of lessees in the Public Market in compliance with Section 112 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 7 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 1, Volume I; c. require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the general fund for the operation of public market in compliance with Section 313 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 1.3.1 of the Manual on Setting up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprise; and d. instruct the Municipal Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer to locate all missing original contracts and demand/collection letters; consistently and uniformly send demand/collection letters to delinquent tenants and strictly impose the provisions of the contract.
observations.managementAction: The Market Administrator is locating the missing contracts and the Municipal Accountant recorded the unpaid rent from delinquent accounts in the Public Market as of December 2019.
observations.reason: Not all contracts were presented yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct all departments/offices including barangays to formulate their Gender and Development Plan and Budget (GPB) proposal and Gender and Development (GAD) Accomplishment Report (AR) of the previous year for the preparation and consolidation of LGU GPB and GAD AR by the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) and Technical Working Group (TWG); b. instruct the GAD Focal Person and the TWG to review the examples of expenses that can be charged and cannot be charged to the GAD Budget as enumerated in Annex D of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2016-01: Amendments to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 (Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women) in the preparation and consolidation of the GAD Plan and Budget and its full utilization; and c. ensure that all members of the GFPS undergo capacity building programs on GAD which shall include but not limited to gender sensitivity training (GST), gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and GAD tools to become effective and efficient in mainstreaming GAD perspectives in LGU programs, projects, activities and processes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is formulating the GAD Plan as recommended. Expenses are based on the approved GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.reason: Some activities were affected by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the Due from LGU account of the Municipality with the corresponding reciprocal account in the books of the Province and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments. Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant request the Provincial Accountant to furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers showing the amount collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due from LGU in the books of the Municipality as provided under Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The reciprocal accounts were tallied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) exert extra effort in securing all pertinent documents to facilitate the registration and transfer of the title of the land properties from the previous owners to the Municipality of Maragondon; b) conduct physical inventory of all land properties owned by the Municipality to properly identify them individually and to facilitate reconciliation between the records of the Property Officer and Accounting Department; c) secure tax assessment from the BIR to know the transfer taxes due and for the Municipality to allot budget to facilitate payment of the same; and d) make disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as regards the status of the titling of the acquired/donated parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: Not Reiterated in Implemented Observation No. 7, Part II of this report.
observations.reason: Management has yet to fully secure all documents for the facilitation of transfer of title of the land.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that collections from
RPT/SET Receivables were properly recorded in the books in
compliance with Sections 19.b and 20, Chapter 3, Volume I of the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local
Government Units (LGUs); and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to rectify and reconstruct the
incorrect entries made in CY 2019 and also in previous years, if
applicable, on transactions involving collections of Real Property Taxes
(RPT) and Special Education Taxes (SET) applying the recommended
correcting entries provided in Annex 6 and Annex 7.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant to work back and recognize all
Infrastructure Assets of the Municipality as well as major repairs
relating to buildings and other structures as part of the Property, Plant
and Equipment (PPE) account in compliance with PPSAS No. 17 by
preparing all the necessary Adjusting Journal Entries;
b. require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal
Engineer, to derecognize replaced parts of the Infrastructure Assets
and Buildings and Other Structures in accordance with PPSAS No. 17
(paragraphs 82-87); and
c. require the Municipal Accountant to henceforth religiously record,
evaluate and qualify items to be recognized as part of the PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant to observe the perpetual inventory
method in recording purchases and issuances in compliance with the
provisions of Section 114 of Chapter 7 of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS), Volume I and Section 44 of
Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 12;
b. require the Inventory Custodians of office supplies and drugs and
medicines to submit summary of supplies/drugs and medicines issued
weekly and report of physical count of inventories every July 31 and
January 31 as provided in Chapter 7 of the MNGAS; and
c. require the Municipal Accountant to henceforth prepare the necessary
Journal Entry Voucher to record the balances of inventories on hand as
of December 31, 2019.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council
(LDRRMC) to facilitate the preparation of complete Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) covering
current year LDRRMF and unexpended balances from the previous
years; and
b. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry
voucher to revert back to the general fund the unutilized LDDRMF
balances for Calendar Years 2013 and 2014 to be used for social
services projects, programs and activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer to refrain from obligating and charging LDRRMF for expenditures which
are not directly related to the implementation of disaster risk management
activities and ensure strict compliance with the guidelines set forth under
NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1. It is
recommended that administrative and operating expenses be charged instead
against the respective General Fund appropriations of the office concerned as
provided under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1
dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to record the PPE in the books only after
delivery of the items purchased;
b. instruct the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to
continue their efforts in reconciling the noted variances to establish the
correct book balances of the PPE accounts; and
c. require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting
journal entries to correct the balances of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate bonding
of the Inventory Storekeeper and Property Officer, in compliance with the
provisions of Section 101 of PD 1445 and Sec. 4.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009
dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Market Administrator/Municipal Treasurer to submit the
Schedule of Tenants with delinquent accounts to the Municipal
Accountant;
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to pass the necessary Journal Entry
Voucher based on the submitted schedule of delinquent accounts in
compliance with Section 112 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and
Section 7 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards
(PPSAS) No. 1, Volume I;
c. require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the
general fund for the operation of public market in compliance with
Section 313 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 1.3.1 of the Manual on
Setting up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprise; and
d. instruct the Municipal Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer to
locate all missing original contracts and demand/collection letters;
consistently and uniformly send demand/collection letters to delinquent
tenants and strictly impose the provisions of the contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct all departments/offices including barangays to formulate their
Gender and Development Plan and Budget (GPB) proposal and Gender
and Development (GAD) Accomplishment Report (AR) of the previous
year for the preparation and consolidation of LGU GPB and GAD AR by
 the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Technical Working Group (TWG);
b. instruct the GAD Focal Person and the TWG to review the examples of
expenses that can be and cannot be charged to the GAD Budget as
enumerated in Annex D of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum
Circular (JMC) No. 2016-01: Amendments to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC
No. 2013-01 (Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of
Women) in the preparation and consolidation of the GAD Plan and
Budget and its full utilization; and
c. ensure that all members of the GFPS undergo capacity building
programs on GAD which shall include but not limited to gender
sensitivity training (GST), gender-responsive planning and budgeting,
gender analysis, gender audit and GAD tools to become effective and
efficient in mainstreaming GAD perspectives in LGU programs,
projects, activities and processes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy
in the Due from LGU account of the Municipality with the corresponding
reciprocal account in the books of the Province and thereafter, make the
necessary adjustments.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant request the
Provincial Accountant to furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers
showing the amount collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to serve
as basis in recording the Due from LGU in the books of the Municipality as
provided under Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended
that the Municipal Mayor:
a) require the Inventory Team to facilitate the conduct of complete physical count of all PPE to prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to ascertain the existence, accuracy, validity and to confirm the condition of the recorded PPEs; and
b) require the Accounting Department and General Services Office to facilitate PPE reconciliation between the accounting and property records.
observations.managementAction:
The General Services Office (GSO) conducted complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to ascertain the existence, accuracy, validity and to confirm the condition of the recorded PPEs and
The Municipal Accountant has reconciled the variances in some items in PPE account.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant has reconciled the variances in some items in PPE account. The management has yet to fully reconcile the variances on PPE account.
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) adopt additional measures like deputizing the barangay treasurers, conducting Business One Stop Shop and caravans and the other strategies to improve the collection efficiency of the Municipality of Maragondon and to prevent the continued yearly increase/accumulation of uncollected RPT and SET receivables; b) conduct intensive/aggressive tax campaign and information drive in order to encourage/invite more taxpayers to pay their taxes; and c) fill-up vacant positions or augment the number of personnel in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to help in the Municipality’s collection efforts.
observations.managementAction: The Management has activated Collection Task Force and has conducted Business One Stop Shop and caravans and the Implementation of Municipal Tax Ordinance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant to work back and recognize all Infrastructure Assets of the Municipality as well as major repairs relating to buildings and other structures as part of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) account in compliance with PPSAS No. 17 by preparing all the necessary Adjusting Journal Entries; b. require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to derecognize replaced parts of the Infrastructure Assets and Buildings and Other Structures in accordance with PPSAS No. 17 (paragraphs 82-87); and c. require the Municipal Accountant to henceforth religiously record, evaluate and qualify items to be recognized as part of the PPE account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant followed the recommendation given by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) exert extra effort in securing all pertinent documents to facilitate the transfer of title of the properties from the previous owners to the Municipality of Maragondon; b) conduct physical inventory of all land properties owned by the Municipality to properly identify them individually and to facilitate reconciliation between the records of the Property Officer and Accounting Department; c) secure tax assessment from the BIR to know the transfer taxes due and for the Municipality to allot budget to facilitate payment of the same; and d) make disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as regards the status of the titling of the acquired/donated parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: The Management has secured Certificate of registration and some land documents for the facilitation of transfer of title of the land.
observations.reason: Management has yet to fully assess the transfer taxes due.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality fully utilize the 20 per cent Development Fund and implement the remaining projects or reprogram those that are not intended so that the beneficiaries could derive the optimum benefits from the Fund.
observations.managementAction: Implemented all PPA’s of 20 per cent Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) direct the Human Resource Officer, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the existing Civil Service Commission (CSC) rules and regulations in processing application of monetization of leave credits of the officials and employees of the Municipality; b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to pass the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify future monetization of leave credits expenses from Other Personnel Benefits account to Terminal Leave Benefits account; c) require the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budget for the monetary value of earned leave credits of the officers and employees; and d) instruct the Municipal Accountant to pass the necessary JEV for the provision of the monetary value of earned leave credits.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource Officer has instructed the concerned personnel to strictly adhere with the audit recommendation given.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
- direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and GAD Focal Person to establish and maintain a GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and
- direct the Municipal Budget Officer and GAD Focal Person to ensure that only PAPs that address the identified GAD issues and concerns be included and charged to the GAD Budget.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal management has already established the database for GAD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account in the General Fund for the Kawayang Maragondon Economic Enterprise (KMEE) through the use of complete subsidiary ledger to generate financial statements, in order to be able to generate financial statements that would show whether the economic enterprise is viable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has continuously records or separate ledger/ books for the financial position and performance of Kawayang Maragondon and prepared a complete set of subsidiary ledger in order to be able to generate financial statements that would show whether the economic enterprise is viable.
observations.reason: Management has yet to fully monitor the financial position and performance of KMEE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records with the Treasury and make the necessary adjustment to reflect the correct balance of Real Property Tax (RPT) and Special Education Tax (SET) receivables based on the certified list of taxpayers submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide the necessary assistance for the sustainability of their Kawayan Maragondon Economic Enterprise that could create potential job opportunities to their constituent.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Business Permit and Licensing Office to require the business taxpayers to submit sworn statement of gross receipts/sales for all their transactions within and outside of the Municipality for the preceding year and other pertinent records for the determination of their gross receipts; and
the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Business Permit and Licensing Office to assess the gross receipts and validate the Business Tax Payable of P565,351.01 and enforce the collection of underpayment, pursuant to the rates provided in the 2014 Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality.  Absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection of P565,351.01 shall be issued with corresponding Notice of Charge.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer to submit justification/basis on the computation of ABC at 10% for evaluation of the Audit Team.  We also recommended adopt Section 22 of Revenue Regulation             No. 4-2007 issued by the Bureau of Internal Revenue in the preparation of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) to the advantage of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and GAD Focal Person to establish and maintain GAD database as required under Item 4.B.1 of the JMC No. 2013-01.  Then, analyze and assess the database/ sex disaggregated data and identify the priority gender issues that needs to be addressed.
We also recommended Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Budget Officer and GAD Focal Person to ensure that only PPAs that address the identified GAD issues and concerns be included and charged to the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor seek prior approval from the Secretary of Budget and Management for their subsequent acquisition of motor vehicle enumerated in Item 3.1 of the said DILG MC, sourced from the National Government Funds under the General Appropriations Act.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials and employees to settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances within the prescribed period/year-end in compliance with Item 5.8 of COA Circular        No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to return/ remit to the sourced agency any unused balance of completed and unimplemented projects or request approval for the use of said fund for other purpose.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to assess whether these payables outstanding for more than two (2) have actual claims, judicial or administrative, has been filed.  Otherwise, revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Service Officer and the Secretary of Bids and Awards Committee to submit its APP on or before November 30 of each year to the Procurement Service of the Department of Budget Management using the modified format as provided under the DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 to ensure the inclusion of the Municipality’s requirements for common-use supplies and equipment and assist the DBM in projecting the inventory requirement of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger and financial statements on the operation of the Economic Enterprise in order to determine whether the income generated is sufficient to meet the operating cost and to provide adequate information as to assets, liabilities and equity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer to coordinate, evaluate and address the discrepancy in property balances between the accounting records and inventory report of P22.337 million.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor advice the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation expense for all PPEs recorded in the books of accounts.

We, likewise, recommended that the Municipal Mayor advice the Municipal General Service Officer (GSO) to coordinate with the Provincial GSO for the costing of the donated property.
observations.managementAction:
General Service Office and the Office of the Municipal Accountant evaluated and address the discrepancy by conducting physical inventory of all properties of the Municipality and prepared the Report on the Physical Count of PPE.  Management also prepared and maintained the property/ledger card.

The Municipal Accountant provide depreciation to all depreciable property of the Municipality.

Management sent a letter requesting information on the donated property.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require Ms. Emma Delas Alas to immediately facilitate the production of duplicate of owner’s copy of title so that the subject parcel of land could be registered under the name of the Municipality, for its protection from third party claims.  

We also recommended that the Municipality refrain from purchasing parcel of land wherein the whereabouts of the transfer certificate of title is unknown.
observations.managementAction: The transferring of the certificate of title under the name of the Municipality is in-process.
observations.reason: The process of transferring the certificate of title entails time.   As of this date, the documents are already in the BIR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustment to reflect the correct balance of Real Property Tax (RPT) and Special Education Tax (SET) Receivables, and Deferred RPT and SET Receivables.  

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the collection of RPT and SET in order to ensure proper distribution of share of the RPT and SET.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant made an adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of RPT and SET Receivables.

The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant made a monthly reconciliation on the distribution of share on the collection of RPT.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget and Accountant to adjust the salary of the LGU personnel to its original salary before the implementation of the 4th tranche salary adjustment, and cause the refund of P2.567 million representing increase in salary.
observations.managementAction:
Management adjusted the rate of the salary of its officials and employees before the implementation of the 4th tranche salary adjustment.

The refund of salary increase is on-going.
observations.reason: The refund of salary increase is not yet finished.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to stop the collection of BAC fee in compliance with DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A.  

We also recommended that the remaining balance of P51,910.70 arising from the sale of bidding documents be transferred from Trust Fund to the General Fund under the Trust Liability account per Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).
observations.managementAction:
Management stop the collection of BAC fee in compliance with the said DBM Budget Circular.

Management transferred the balance of P51,910.70 arising from the sale of bidding documents to Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Health Officer to maximize the utilization of the Per Family Payment Rate (PFPR) as required under Philhealth Circular No. 010, s. 2012 for the promotion of quality health care services.
observations.managementAction: Funds for PCB-1 granted by Philhealth has been utilized by procuring necessary drugs and medicines.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advice the Municipal Health Officer to immediately implement the priority health projects, plans and activities of the Municipality in order that benefits are availed of by the intended beneficiaries at the earliest possible time.  

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor advice the Municipal Health Officer to submit the work and financial plan to the Audit Team for evaluation.
observations.managementAction:
Programs for health projects has been implemented.

Municipal Health Officer submitted the work and financial plan for health program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advice the Municipal Accountant to deduct 10% retention money on all progress billing until 50% of the value of the works, are completed, or require the contractor to post an irrevocable standby letter of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds callable on demand, of amount equivalent to the retention money provided that the project is on schedule and is satisfactorily undertaken as required under Annex “E” of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management deducted 10% retention money on progress billing until 50% of the value of the work.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advice the Municipal Accountant to require the Contractor to post the necessary warranty security required under Section 62 of the IRR-A of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Contractors posted the required warranty security of their infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to post all Notices of Award together with the Notices to Proceed and the approved contracts for the procurement of infrastructure projects and goods and services in the PhilGEPS in compliance with the above-cited regulation.
observations.managementAction: Notice of award has been posted at the PhilGEPS website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advice the Bids and Awards Committee to invite in all stages of procurement process, in writing, a representative of COA Audit Team and representatives from a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand, and from a non-government organization in compliance with Section 13 of the    IRR-A of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management invited observers in the stages of bidding

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advice the General Service Officer strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the procurement and issuance of supplies and materials as prescribed under Chapter VII, Vol. I of the Manual on NGAS.
observations.managementAction: Management use perpetual inventory method in the recording of procurement of supplies and materials

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advice the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the completion and approval of its Local Government Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan as required under       RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality for the period of 2015-2014 has already been approved by the National Solid Waste Management Committee.
